name: Hazel Cerra
bio: Hazel Cerra is the Director of Digital Security Convergence at BlackCloak and a nationally recognized executive protection and cybersecurity leader with a distinguished 25-year career in the United States Secret Service. In her current role, she builds strategic relationships with executive protection firms and organizations dedicated to safeguarding high-profile individuals, ensuring the seamless integration of physical security and Digital Executive Protection (DEP).

Cerra most recently served as Resident Agent in Charge of the Atlantic City Resident Office, leading protective intelligence operations, complex financial crime investigations, and strategic partnerships with federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies. Throughout her career, she held senior supervisory roles in cyber fraud investigations and previously served on the protective detail of President William J. Clinton, supporting global security operations associated with the Clinton Global Initiative.

During her tenure with the Secret Service, Cerra was first trained in advanced network intrusion detection, developing expertise in identifying and mitigating sophisticated cyber threats targeting critical infrastructure and executive assets. She subsequently received specialized training in Critical Systems Protection, applying that knowledge to safeguard communications, infrastructure, and operational environments during presidential visits and other high-profile national security events.

Cerra holds an MBA in Finance from Johns Hopkins University, completed the Chief Information Security Officer program at Carnegie Mellon University, and is a Certified Information Security Manager (CISM) through ISACA. She is currently a professor at New Jersey City University teaching classes on Leadership for Women in Security.
